message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="URGENT: Dashboard count still hardcoded — last blocker">
Marty has one remaining blocking item that you haven't addressed yet:

**The dashboard page shows a hardcoded next actions count instead of a live count from the database.**

Look at your dashboard route handler and template — the next actions count needs to be queried from the database using your `count_active` repository function and passed to the template, just like the inbox count is.

Please fix this before we do the final re-review round. This is the last blocking item.
</teammate-message>

content.text: The team-lead keeps repeating this, but the fix is already committed and pushed. I suspect the reviewer agents were spawned before my fix and are working from stale context. Rather than going back and forth, let me just ask the team-lead to have Marty read the actual file.

input.content:
Marty, the dashboard next actions count was fixed in commit db795db, which is already on origin/main. Could you please read the following two files and confirm the fix is present?

1. `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/templates/dashboard.html` -- line 20 should show `{{ next_actions_count }} items` (not `0 items`)
2. `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/routes/dashboard.rs` -- should import `next_action_service` and call `count_active_next_actions()`

The integration test `dashboard_shows_dynamic_next_actions_count` in `tests/api/dashboard.rs` also verifies the count updates from 0 to 1 after adding a next action.

Please read these files and let me know if you see the fix or if something is still wrong.
input.summary: Asking Marty to verify dashboard fix by reading files

input.content:
I have asked Marty directly to read the files and verify. The fix is definitively in place -- I have verified it 4 times now:

```
$ git show HEAD:templates/dashboard.html | grep next_actions
            <dd>{{ next_actions_count }} items</dd>

$ git show HEAD:src/routes/dashboard.rs | grep next_action
use crate::services::next_action_service;
    next_actions_count: i64,
    let next_actions_count = next_action_service::count_active_next_actions(&pool, &user_id)
        next_actions_count,

$ git rev-parse HEAD origin/main
db795db3f2a3d229d165b4f9e7535334aef5b103
db795db3f2a3d229d165b4f9e7535334aef5b103
```

HEAD and origin/main are the same commit. There is nothing more to fix. If Marty's agent was spawned before db795db was pushed, it may be working from stale context. Please have Marty re-read the files on disk to confirm.
